mnemonics.title: MVP Transformation Order
mnemonics.content: MOM VPO: Model → Object → World, then View transforms to Camera, then Projection to Image, then Output to screen. Remember: 'My Very Petulant Octopus' style - Model View Projection Order (MVP)

mnemonics.title: Frustum Planes
mnemonics.content: 6 planes clip: Near, Far, Left, Right, Top, Bottom - Remember as 'Never Forget Left Right Top Bottom' for the frustum boundaries.

mnemonics.title: Camera Coordinate System
mnemonics.content: X points Right, Y points Up, Z points Into scene (Right-handed). Remember: 'XYZ = Right, Up, In'

acronyms.usage: Angular extent of visible scene, determines how much of the 3D world is captured
Standardized coordinate system after projection, range [-1,1] in x and y
The three main transformations applied to 3D geometry in rendering pipeline
Function that creates a perspective projection viewing frustum

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a pyramid with the tip cut off - the camera is at the narrow end (apex), looking through the wider base. Near plane is the front cut, far plane is the back cut.
Perspective: railroad tracks converging at horizon. Orthographic: architect's blueprint where parallel lines stay parallel.
Think of camera as a flashlight (view volume) shining on a wall (image plane) - only what's in the beam appears on the wall.
Like a movie editor cutting scenes outside the camera frame - only what's within the framing rectangle makes it to the final image.

analogies.analogy: Like positioning a physical camera on a tripod - you choose where to place it (position), where to point it (look-at), and which way is up (up vector).
Like the focus range of your eyes - things too close are blurry (clipped), things too far fade into darkness (far plane).
Like the difference between a telescope (small FOV, magnified view) and a wide-angle lens (large FOV, more scene but distorted edges).
Like objects appearing smaller when further away - standing near a tree makes it look huge, same tree far away looks tiny.
